sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with right ankle pain and swelling after falling on ice yesterday.
He reports being cautious while walking but slipped and fell, resulting in the injury.
Initially, he was unable to walk on the affected ankle, but after icing and elevating it, he has experienced some improvement, although he is still limping.
He has taken ibuprofen for pain relief, which has provided some benefit.
He denies any numbness in the foot.
He has a history of playing soccer and has had previous injuries, but he is unsure if he has ever injured this specific ankle before.
His children are also involved in soccer, with his oldest son about to start playing in a league.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen as needed for pain relief.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are within normal limits.
Physical exam: On physical examination, there is ecchymosis and bruising on the skin over the lateral malleolus associated with swelling.
Tenderness is present to palpation anterolaterally in the soft tissue, but there is no laxity on anterior drawer and inversion stress, and no bony tenderness on palpation of the foot.
The neurovascular exam of the right foot shows a capillary refill of less than three seconds with a strong dorsalis pedis pulse and intact sensation to light touch.
Test Results: The right ankle x-ray showed no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with a right ankle sprain.
Problem list: Right ankle sprain; status: confirmed.
Plan:
I would like the patient to keep the leg elevated, especially when seated, and continue to ice it.
An aircast will be provided to help stabilize the ankle, and crutches will be given for the next one to two days.
The patient may start walking on it as tolerated.
The patient will continue to take NSAIDs as needed for pain relief.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in two weeks to assess the progression of symptoms and determine readiness to return to soccer.
